title: Crepidomanes latealatum
intro: Crepidomanes latealatum is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the late-veined filmy fern. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Crepidomanes latealatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Crepidomanes latealatum is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family is commonly known as the filmy fern family. It is a part of the larger group called Polypodiopsida, which includes all the ferns and their allies. Crepidomanes latealatum is also classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all the vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The species is further classified under the kingdom Plantae and the domain Eukaryota.

preparations: Crepidomanes latealatum, a type of fern native to New Zealand, has been used in traditional Maori herbal preparations.
